title: daviesia hakeoides subsp. subnuda
intro: Daviesia hakeoides subsp. subnuda, also known as the Hakea-leaved Daviesia or subnuda Daviesia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Western Australia, where it can be found growing in well-drained soils in woodland and heathland areas. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits, including the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Daviesia hakeoides subsp. subnuda is often used as a topical treatment, where it is applied directly to the affected area in the form of an ointment or salve.
